paper_title: LaMDA: Language Models for Dialog Applications

raw_extraction.innovation: Fine-tuning with crowdworker-annotated data improves safety, groundedness, and quality metrics. The model learns to consult external knowledge sources (e.g., information retrieval, calculator, translator) to generate factually grounded responses
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: 1.56T words from public dialog data and web texts
raw_extraction.application: Dialog applications, education, content recommendations, instruction following
raw_extraction.research_problem: Improving safety, factual grounding, and quality in dialog models through scalable pre-training and targeted fine-tuning
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Discriminative fine-tuning for quality, safety, and groundedness; generation of tool calls to external knowledge sources
raw_extraction.extension: Model is fine-tuned to call an external information retrieval system (TS) to verify claims and generate grounded responses during inference
